title: The Varns'rama System: the Withdrawn and the Renounced
summary: Krishna teaches Uddhava about witness consciousness, detachment from family/objects, and devotion (Bhakti). Addresses relationship endings, dealing with grief, letting go of attachments, and mental calm.

sections.summary: This passage begins with “The Supreme Lord said: 'When one in the third phase of one's life wants to retreat into the forest, one should, in order to reside there peacefully, entrust the wife to one's sons or else…” and develops toward “He should move about alone on this earth free from attachment, with his senses fully under control, and, satisfied and amused with the True Self,…”

This passage begins with “In a remote and safe place the sage, with his consciousness purified by his love for Me, should concentrate on the soul only as being non-different from Me.” and develops toward “He though who is not in control of the six forms [of vice, the anarthas], who allows the charioteer of the body to be led by the senses, is bereft of…”

This passage begins with “He though who is not in control of the six forms [of vice, the anarthas], who allows the charioteer of the body to be led by the senses, is bereft of knowledge and detachment, and just…” and develops toward “Oh saintly soul, this what I described to you on your request, constitutes the means by which one as a devotee can be perfectly engaged in one's…”

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच वनं विविक्षुः पुत्रेषु भार्यां न्यस्य सहैव वा । वन एव वसेच् छान्तस् तृतीयं भागम् आयुषः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca vanaṃ vivikṣuḥ putreṣu bhāryāṃ nyasya sahaiva vā / vana eva vasec chāntas tṛtīyaṃ bhāgam āyuṣaḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'When one in the third phase of one's life wants to retreat into the forest, one should, in order to reside there peacefully, entrust the wife to one's sons or else together with her dwell in the forest.

verses.sanskrit: कन्द-मूल-फलैर् वन्यैर् मेध्यैर् वृत्तिं प्रकल्पयेत् । वसीत वल्कलं वासस् तृण-पर्णाजिनानि वा
verses.transliteration: kanda-mūla-phalair vanyair medhyair vṛttiṃ prakalpayet / vasīta valkalaṃ vāsas tṛṇa-parṇājināni vā
verses.translation: One should arrange for the pure [*] sustenance of the bulbs, roots and fruits of the forest, and dress oneself with tree bark, grass, leaves or animal skins.

verses.sanskrit: केश-रोम-नख-श्मश्रु- मलानि बिभृयाद् दतः । न धावेद् अप्सु मज्जेत त्रि कालं स्थण्डिले-शयः
verses.transliteration: keśa-roma-nakha-śmaśru- malāni bibhṛyād dataḥ / na dhāved apsu majjeta tri kālaṃ sthaṇḍile-śayaḥ
verses.translation: [In the forest] one should allow the hair on one's head and body, one's facial hair and nails [to grow], as also the filth of one's body, not [extensively] clean one's teeth, [but] bathe three times a day and [at night] sleep on the ground.

verses.sanskrit: ग्रीष्मे तप्येत पञ्चाग्नीन् वर्षास्व् आसार-षाड् जले । आकण्थ-मग्नः शिशिर एवं वृत्तस् तपश् चरेत्
verses.transliteration: grīṣme tapyeta pañcāgnīn varṣāsv āsāra-ṣāḍ jale / ākaṇtha-magnaḥ śiśira evaṃ vṛttas tapaś caret
verses.translation: Thus proceeding one must ascetically tolerate the five fires during the summer [the sacrificial fires in four directions and the sun above], the torrents of rain during the rainy season, and the cold of winter when one is submerged in water up to one's neck [see also 4.23: 6].

verses.sanskrit: अग्नि-पक्वं समश्नीयात् काल-पक्वम् अथापि वा । उलूखलाश्म-कुट्टो वा दन्तोलूखल एव वा
verses.transliteration: agni-pakvaṃ samaśnīyāt kāla-pakvam athāpi vā / ulūkhalāśma-kuṭṭo vā dantolūkhala eva vā
verses.translation: One eats what is either prepared on a fire, what has ripened by time or what was pulverized with a mortar, with a stone or ground with one's teeth.

verses.sanskrit: स्वयं सञ्चिनुयात् सर्वम् आत्मनो वृत्ति-कारणम् । देश-काल-बलाभिज्ञो नाददीतान्यदाहृतम्
verses.transliteration: svayaṃ sañcinuyāt sarvam ātmano vṛtti-kāraṇam / deśa-kāla-balābhijño nādadītānyadāhṛtam
verses.translation: One should personally collect whatever that is needed for one's sustenance depending the place, the time and one's energy, and understand that [living in the forest] one must not store anything for another time [see also 7.12: 19].

verses.sanskrit: वन्यैश् चरु-पुरोडाशैर् निर्वपेत् काल-चोदितान् । न तु श्रौतेन पशुना मां यजेत वनाश्रमी
verses.transliteration: vanyaiś caru-puroḍāśair nirvapet kāla-coditān / na tu śrautena paśunā māṃ yajeta vanāśramī
verses.translation: A vnaprastha may choose for oblations [of rice, barley and dl], may offer rice cakes or offer fruits according to the season, but he may never worship Me with sacrificing animals, [even though it is] mentioned in the scriptures.

verses.sanskrit: अग्निहोत्रं च दर्शश् च पौर्णमासश् च पूर्व-वत् । चातुर्मास्यानि च मुनेर् आम्नातानि च नैगमैः
verses.transliteration: agnihotraṃ ca darśaś ca paurṇamāsaś ca pūrva-vat / cāturmāsyāni ca muner āmnātāni ca naigamaiḥ
verses.translation: As he did before [when he was a grihastha] he should perform the fire sacrifice, the sacrifice on a new moon day and a full moon day, as also the four months' sacrifice [of cturmsya], that are enjoined by the Vedic experts.

verses.sanskrit: एवं चीर्णेन तपसा मुनिर् धमनि-सन्ततः । मां तपो-मयम् आराध्य ऋषि-लोकाद् उपैति माम्
verses.transliteration: evaṃ cīrṇena tapasā munir dhamani-santataḥ / māṃ tapo-mayam ārādhya ṛṣi-lokād upaiti mām
verses.translation: When he with that ascetic practice has worshiped Me, the sage [the vnaprastha] all skin and bones, from the world of the seers will achieve Me, the Goal of All Penance [see also maharloka].

verses.sanskrit: यस् त्व् एतत् कृच्छ्रतश् चीर्णं तपो निःश्रेयसं महत् । कामायाल्पीयसे युञ्ज्याद् बालिशः को ऽपरस् ततः
verses.transliteration: yas tv etat kṛcchrataś cīrṇaṃ tapo niḥśreyasaṃ mahat / kāmāyālpīyase yuñjyād bāliśaḥ ko 'paras tataḥ
verses.translation: Is there a greater fool than someone who, for a long time being of this difficult but glorious penance which awards liberation, practices this penance for the purpose of trivial sense gratification [see also vnts']?

verses.sanskrit: यदासौ नियमे ऽकल्पो जरया जात-वेपथुः । आत्मन्य् अग्नीन् समारोप्य मच्-चित्तो ऽग्निं समाविशेत्
verses.transliteration: yadāsau niyame 'kalpo jarayā jāta-vepathuḥ / ātmany agnīn samāropya mac-citto 'gniṃ samāviśet
verses.translation: When he in his regulated activities due to old age with his body trembling is no longer able to carry on, he should place the sacrificial fire in his heart, concentrate on Me and enter the fire [see also 7.12: 23].

verses.sanskrit: यदा कर्म-विपाकेषु लोकेषु निरयात्मसु । विरागो जायते सम्यङ् न्यस्ताग्निः प्रव्रजेत् ततः
verses.transliteration: yadā karma-vipākeṣu lokeṣu nirayātmasu / virāgo jāyate samyaṅ nyastāgniḥ pravrajet tataḥ
verses.translation: But in case full detachment from all result-minded action has developed in him and [the achievement of] a higher world means nothing more to him than hell, he may give up the [vnaprastha] sacrificial fire and take to the renounced order [see also B.G. 18: 2 and **].

verses.sanskrit: इष्ट्वा यथोपदेशं मां दत्त्वा सर्व-स्वम् ऋत्विजे । अग्नीन् स्व-प्राण आवेश्य निरपेक्षः परिव्रजेत्
verses.transliteration: iṣṭvā yathopadeśaṃ māṃ dattvā sarva-svam ṛtvije / agnīn sva-prāṇa āveśya nirapekṣaḥ parivrajet
verses.translation: After having worshiped Me according to the injunctions and having given all he has to the priest, he [the vnaprastha] must place the sacrificial fire within his life breath and free from desires and worries wander about [as a sannys, see also 9.6*].

verses.sanskrit: विप्रस्य वै सन्न्यसतो देवा दारादि-रूपिणः । विघ्नान् कुर्वन्त्य् अयं ह्य् अस्मान् आक्रम्य समियात् परम्
verses.transliteration: viprasya vai sannyasato devā dārādi-rūpiṇaḥ / vighnān kurvanty ayaṃ hy asmān ākramya samiyāt param
verses.translation: The demigods to begin with create obstacles for him in the form of his wife [and other allurements later on]; these he should surpass and transcend [see also B.G. 6: 25, 1.19: 2-3, 5.6: 4, 11.4: 7].

verses.sanskrit: बिभृयाच् चेन् मुनिर् वासः कौपीनाच्छादनं परम् । त्यक्तं न दण्ड-पात्राभ्याम् अन्यत् किञ्चिद् अनापदि
verses.transliteration: bibhṛyāc cen munir vāsaḥ kaupīnācchādanaṃ param / tyaktaṃ na daṇḍa-pātrābhyām anyat kiñcid anāpadi
verses.translation: If a sage wishes to wear clothes he uses another cloth to cover his loincloth [or kaupna]. All the rest he gives up, besides a water pot and a staff [accepting] nothing else without necessity.

verses.sanskrit: दृष्टि-पूतं न्यसेत् पादं वस्त्र-पूतं पिबेज् जलम् । सत्य-पूतां वदेद् वाचं मनः-पूतं समाचरेत्
verses.transliteration: dṛṣṭi-pūtaṃ nyaset pādaṃ vastra-pūtaṃ pibej jalam / satya-pūtāṃ vaded vācaṃ manaḥ-pūtaṃ samācaret
verses.translation: He should place his foot on a clear place [free from living beings], clear the water he wants to drink with his cloth, speak the truth in clear terms and engage in action with a clear mind.

verses.sanskrit: मौनानीहानिलायामा दण्डा वाग्-देह-चेतसाम् । न ह्य् एते यस्य सन्त्य् अङ्ग वेणुभिर् न भवेद् यतिः
verses.transliteration: maunānīhānilāyāmā daṇḍā vāg-deha-cetasām / na hy ete yasya santy aṅga veṇubhir na bhaved yatiḥ
verses.translation: Saying little, reserve and breath control constitute the strict disciplines for the voice, the body and the mind. He with whom these are not found My dearest, can, despite his bamboo rods, never be considered a real sannys [see also tridanda].

verses.sanskrit: भिक्षां चतुर्षु वर्णेषु विगर्ह्यान् वर्जयंश् चरेत् । सप्तागारान् असङ्कॢप्तांस् तुष्येल् लब्धेन तावता
verses.transliteration: bhikṣāṃ caturṣu varṇeṣu vigarhyān varjayaṃś caret / saptāgārān asaṅkḷptāṃs tuṣyel labdhena tāvatā
verses.translation: When he goes begging with the four varnas he should at random approach seven different houses, avoid impure [sinful, polluted] households and be satisfied with whatever he acquired [see also cakra, compare 1.4: 8].

verses.sanskrit: बहिर् जलाशयं गत्वा तत्रोपस्पृश्य वाग्-यतः । विभज्य पावितं शेषं भुञ्जीताशेषम् आहृतम्
verses.transliteration: bahir jalāśayaṃ gatvā tatropaspṛśya vāg-yataḥ / vibhajya pāvitaṃ śeṣaṃ bhuñjītāśeṣam āhṛtam
verses.translation: He next somewhere outside has to go to a reservoir of water, wash himself and then without saying a word distribute the food that was begged and purified. Thereafter he should eat the food that remained in its entirety.

verses.sanskrit: एकश् चरेन् महीम् एतां निःसङ्गः संयतेन्द्रियः । आत्म-क्रीड आत्म-रत आत्म-वान् सम-दर्शनः
verses.transliteration: ekaś caren mahīm etāṃ niḥsaṅgaḥ saṃyatendriyaḥ / ātma-krīḍa ātma-rata ātma-vān sama-darśanaḥ
verses.translation: He should move about alone on this earth free from attachment, with his senses fully under control, and, satisfied and amused with the True Self, steady on the spiritual platform, be of an equal vision [B.G. 5: 18, see bhajan Ohe! Vaishnava Thhkura].

verses.sanskrit: विविक्त-क्षेम-शरणो मद्-भाव-विमलाशयः । आत्मानं चिन्तयेद् एकम् अभेदेन मया मुनिः
verses.transliteration: vivikta-kṣema-śaraṇo mad-bhāva-vimalāśayaḥ / ātmānaṃ cintayed ekam abhedena mayā muniḥ
verses.translation: In a remote and safe place the sage, with his consciousness purified by his love for Me, should concentrate on the soul only as being non-different from Me.

verses.sanskrit: अन्वीक्षेतात्मनो बन्धं मोक्षं च ज्ञान-निष्ठया । बन्ध इन्द्रिय-विक्षेपो मोक्ष एषां च संयमः
verses.transliteration: anvīkṣetātmano bandhaṃ mokṣaṃ ca jñāna-niṣṭhayā / bandha indriya-vikṣepo mokṣa eṣāṃ ca saṃyamaḥ
verses.translation: By focussing in knowledge he must gain insight in the bound and liberated state of the self. He is bound when the senses are distracted and he is liberated when he has them under control [when he - devoted to Me - concentrates, meditates and gets absorbed, see also 11.10].

verses.sanskrit: तस्मान् नियम्य षड्-वर्गं मद्-भावेन चरेन् मुनिः । विरक्तः क्षुद्र-कामेभ्यो लब्ध्वात्मनि सुखं महत्
verses.transliteration: tasmān niyamya ṣaḍ-vargaṃ mad-bhāvena caren muniḥ / viraktaḥ kṣudra-kāmebhyo labdhvātmani sukhaṃ mahat
verses.translation: The sage with his six senses [the five physical senses and the mind] fully under control being conscious of Me, having experienced the greater happiness of the soul, therefore should live detached from futile material desires.

verses.sanskrit: पुर-ग्राम-व्रजान् सार्थान् भिक्षार्थं प्रविशंश् चरेत् । पुण्य-देश-सरिच्-छैल- वनाश्रम-वतीं महीम्
verses.transliteration: pura-grāma-vrajān sārthān bhikṣārthaṃ praviśaṃś caret / puṇya-deśa-saric-chaila- vanāśrama-vatīṃ mahīm
verses.translation: He should travel to the pure places on earth and travel to the pure places on earth with rivers, mountains and forests. The cities, towns and pasturing grounds he should enter only to beg for alms from those working for material purposes.

verses.sanskrit: वानप्रस्थाश्रम-पदेष्व् अभीक्ष्णं भैक्ष्यम् आचरेत् । संसिध्यत्य् आश्व् असम्मोहः शुद्ध-सत्त्वः शिलान्धसा
verses.transliteration: vānaprasthāśrama-padeṣv abhīkṣṇaṃ bhaikṣyam ācaret / saṃsidhyaty āśv asammohaḥ śuddha-sattvaḥ śilāndhasā
verses.translation: He in the withdrawn position of life should always go for alms, for by food [thus] obtained by begging [and not by performing fruitfu l labor, karma], he is freed from illusion and quickly achieves spiritual perfection.

verses.sanskrit: नैतद् वस्तुतया पश्येद् दृश्यमानं विनश्यति । असक्त-चित्तो विरमेद् इहामुत्र-चिकीर्षितात्
verses.transliteration: naitad vastutayā paśyed dṛśyamānaṃ vinaśyati / asakta-citto viramed ihāmutra-cikīrṣitāt
verses.translation: He should never take the perishable things he sees with his eyes for the ultimate reality; with a consciousness free from attachment he should turn away from all designs for [material progress in] this life or a future existence.

verses.sanskrit: यद् एतद् आत्मनि जगन् मनो-वाक्-प्राण-संहतम् । सर्वं मायेति तर्केण स्व-स्थस् त्यक्त्वा न तत् स्मरेत्
verses.transliteration: yad etad ātmani jagan mano-vāk-prāṇa-saṃhatam / sarvaṃ māyeti tarkeṇa sva-sthas tyaktvā na tat smaret
verses.translation: Fixed in his [true] self he should no longer keep the universe in mind and thus arguing [as previously] forsake all that illusory energy that in the self binds together mind, speech and vital air [see ahankra].

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञान-निष्ठो विरक्तो वा मद्-भक्तो वानपेक्षकः । स-लिङ्गान् आश्रमांस् त्यक्त्वा चरेद् अविधि-गोचरः
verses.transliteration: jñāna-niṣṭho virakto vā mad-bhakto vānapekṣakaḥ / sa-liṅgān āśramāṃs tyaktvā cared avidhi-gocaraḥ
verses.translation: Whether he in detachment is devoted to knowledge or as My devotee not even desires liberation, he [ultimately] should abandon the s'rama duties and their rituals and move beyond the range of rules and regulations [see also 10.78: 31-32, 3.29: 25 and 5.1*].

verses.sanskrit: बुधो बालक-वत् क्रीडेत् कुशलो जड-वच् चरेत् । वदेद् उन्मत्त-वद् विद्वान् गो-चर्यां नैगमश् चरेत्
verses.transliteration: budho bālaka-vat krīḍet kuśalo jaḍa-vac caret / vaded unmatta-vad vidvān go-caryāṃ naigamaś caret
verses.translation: Despite his intelligence he [the detached soul, a sannys] should play like a child, despite his expertise he should act as if incompetent, despite his learning he should speak like being absent-minded and despite his knowing the injunctions he should behave in an unrestricted manner ['wander as a cow'].

verses.sanskrit: वेद-वाद-रतो न स्यान् न पाषण्डी न हैतुकः । शुष्क-वाद-विवादे न कञ्चित् पक्षं समाश्रयेत्
verses.transliteration: veda-vāda-rato na syān na pāṣaṇḍī na haitukaḥ / śuṣka-vāda-vivāde na kañcit pakṣaṃ samāśrayet
verses.translation: He should never attach to the [karma-kanda] Vedic philosophy [of sacrificing for the sake of return], nor should he heretically oppose it; he should refrain from scepticism and empty talk, nor take sides in [political] arguments.

verses.sanskrit: नोद्विजेत जनाद् धीरो जनं चोद्वेजयेन् न तु । अति-वादांस् तितिक्षेत नावमन्येत कञ्चन । देहम् उद्दिश्य पशु-वद् वैरं कुर्यान् न केनचित्
verses.transliteration: nodvijeta janād dhīro janaṃ codvejayen na tu / ati-vādāṃs titikṣeta nāvamanyeta kañcana / deham uddiśya paśu-vad vairaṃ kuryān na kenacit
verses.translation: Someone wise should never feel disturbed by other people nor should he disturb others. He should tolerate harsh words, never treat anyone with disrespect and never - like an animal to the interest of the body - with anyone create hostility [see also B.G. 12: 15].

verses.sanskrit: एक एव परो ह्य् आत्मा भूतेष्व् आत्मन्य् अवस्थितः । यथेन्दुर् उद-पात्रेषु भूतान्य् एकात्मकानि च
verses.transliteration: eka eva paro hy ātmā bhūteṣv ātmany avasthitaḥ / yathendur uda-pātreṣu bhūtāny ekātmakāni ca
verses.translation: The One Supreme Soul is situated within all living beings as also within one's own body. Just like the one moon is reflected in different reservoirs of water, all material bodies are composed of the energy of the One [Lord, see also B.G. 6: 29 & 13: 34].

verses.sanskrit: अलब्ध्वा न विषीदेत काले काले ऽशनं क्वचित् । लब्ध्वा न हृष्येद् धृतिमान् उभयं दैव-तन्त्रितम्
verses.transliteration: alabdhvā na viṣīdeta kāle kāle 'śanaṃ kvacit / labdhvā na hṛṣyed dhṛtimān ubhayaṃ daiva-tantritam
verses.translation: He [the sannys] should not feel dejected when there is no [or no proper] food, nor should he rejoice at times when there is plenty of food; he should be firmly fixed in the notion that both matters [of plenty and scarcity] are determined by fate [by the Lord].

verses.sanskrit: आहारार्थं समीहेत युक्तं तत्-प्राण-धारणम् । तत्त्वं विमृश्यते तेन तद् विज्ञाय विमुच्यते
verses.transliteration: āhārārthaṃ samīheta yuktaṃ tat-prāṇa-dhāraṇam / tattvaṃ vimṛśyate tena tad vijñāya vimucyate
verses.translation: He should endeavor to eat and sustain his personal life force properly, for by that strength he contemplates the spiritual truth that, being understood, leads to liberation [see B.G. 6: 16].

verses.sanskrit: यदृच्छयोपपन्नान्नम् अद्याच् छ्रेष्ठम् उतापरम् । तथा वासस् तथा शय्यां प्राप्तं प्राप्तं भजेन् मुनिः
verses.transliteration: yadṛcchayopapannānnam adyāc chreṣṭham utāparam / tathā vāsas tathā śayyāṃ prāptaṃ prāptaṃ bhajen muniḥ
verses.translation: The food he happened to obtain he must eat, whether it is first class or of a low quality. Similarly the clothes and place to sleep must by a sage be accepted the way they came of their own accord [see also 7.13].

verses.sanskrit: शौचम् आचमनं स्नानं न तु चोदनया चरेत् । अन्यांश् च नियमाञ् ज्ञानी यथाहं लीलयेश्वरः
verses.transliteration: śaucam ācamanaṃ snānaṃ na tu codanayā caret / anyāṃś ca niyamāñ jñānī yathāhaṃ līlayeśvaraḥ
verses.translation: General cleanliness, washing the hands, taking a bath and other regular duties are to be performed without any compulsion by a person of spiritual realization, just as [free as] I, the Controller, act according to the game I play.

verses.sanskrit: न हि तस्य विकल्पाख्या या च मद्-वीक्षया हता । आ-देहान्तात् क्वचित् ख्यातिस् ततः सम्पद्यते मया
verses.transliteration: na hi tasya vikalpākhyā yā ca mad-vīkṣayā hatā / ā-dehāntāt kvacit khyātis tataḥ sampadyate mayā
verses.translation: For him the perception of things [as existing] separate from Me is over, for having Me in view such a perception is destroyed. Sometimes such a notion lingers till the body dies, but he will attain Me then.

verses.sanskrit: दुःखोदर्केषु कामेषु जात-निर्वेद आत्मवान् । अज्ज्ञासित-मद्-धर्मो मुनिं गुरुम् उपव्रजेत्
verses.transliteration: duḥkhodarkeṣu kāmeṣu jāta-nirveda ātmavān / ajjñāsita-mad-dharmo muniṃ gurum upavrajet
verses.translation: He who has no notion of My dharma, but in his desire for spiritual perfection developed detachment from the lusty sense enjoyment that leads to unhappiness, should approach a wise [bonafide] spiritual master [of proper reference, a guru, see also B.G. 16: 23-24, 4: 34 & 17: 14].

verses.sanskrit: तावत् परिचरेद् भक्तः श्रद्धावान् अनसूयकः । यावद् ब्रह्म विजानीयान् माम् एव गुरुम् आदृतः
verses.transliteration: tāvat paricared bhaktaḥ śraddhāvān anasūyakaḥ / yāvad brahma vijānīyān mām eva gurum ādṛtaḥ
verses.translation: The devotee should serve the spiritual master who embodies Me, with great faith without envy and offenses for as long as it takes to arrive at a clear realization of the Absolute Truth [see also 11.17: 27].

verses.sanskrit: यस् त्व् असंयत-षड्-वर्गः प्रचण्डेन्द्रिय-सारथिः । ज्ञान-वैराग्य-रहितस् त्रि-दण्डम् उपजीवति
verses.transliteration: yas tv asaṃyata-ṣaḍ-vargaḥ pracaṇḍendriya-sārathiḥ / jñāna-vairāgya-rahitas tri-daṇḍam upajīvati
verses.translation: He though who is not in control of the six forms [of vice, the anarthas], who allows the charioteer of the body to be led by the senses, is bereft of knowledge and detachment, and just wants to subsist on [and not learn from] the three-stick staff, denies Me, himself and the divine personalities within himself and harms the dharma; not having overcome the contamination of this world he will also be lost and abandoned in the world hereafter.

verses.sanskrit: सुरान् आत्मानम् आत्म-स्थं निह्नुते मां च धर्म-हा । अविपक्व-कषायो ऽस्माद् अमुष्माच् च विहीयते
verses.transliteration: surān ātmānam ātma-sthaṃ nihnute māṃ ca dharma-hā / avipakva-kaṣāyo 'smād amuṣmāc ca vihīyate
verses.translation: He though who is not in control of the six forms [of vice, the anarthas], who allows the charioteer of the body to be led by the senses, is bereft of knowledge and detachment, and just wants to subsist on [and not learn from] the three-stick staff, denies Me, himself and the divine personalities within himself and harms the dharma; not having overcome the contamination of this world he will also be lost and abandoned in the world hereafter.

verses.sanskrit: भिक्षोर् धर्मः शमो ऽहिंसा तप ईक्षा वनौकसः । गृहिणो भूत-रक्षेज्या द्विजस्याचार्य-सेवनम्
verses.transliteration: bhikṣor dharmaḥ śamo 'hiṃsā tapa īkṣā vanaukasaḥ / gṛhiṇo bhūta-rakṣejyā dvijasyācārya-sevanam
verses.translation: Nonviolence and equanimity constitutes the dharma of a mendicant [a sannys], discrimination [between body and soul] and penance constitutes the dharma of someone living in the forest [a vnaprastha], protecting all living beings and performing sacrifices constitutes the dharma of a householder [a grihastha], and serving the teacher of example [the crya] constitutes the dharma of a celibate student [a brahmacr].

verses.sanskrit: ब्रह्मचर्यं तपः शौचं सन्तोषो भूत-सौहृदम् । गृहस्थस्याप्य् ऋतौ गन्तुः सर्वेषां मद्-उपासनम्
verses.transliteration: brahmacaryaṃ tapaḥ śaucaṃ santoṣo bhūta-sauhṛdam / gṛhasthasyāpy ṛtau gantuḥ sarveṣāṃ mad-upāsanam
verses.translation: Of all those who worship Me with celibacy, austerity, cleanliness, contentment and being friendly towards all living entities, it is the householder who may approach his wife for intercourse at the proper time.

verses.sanskrit: इति मां यः स्व-धर्मेण भजेन् नित्यम् अनन्य-भाक् । सर्व-भूतेषु मद्-भावो मद्-भक्तिं विन्दते दृढाम्
verses.transliteration: iti māṃ yaḥ sva-dharmeṇa bhajen nityam ananya-bhāk / sarva-bhūteṣu mad-bhāvo mad-bhaktiṃ vindate dṛḍhām
verses.translation: Someone who thus according to his dharma worships Me, who is not devoted to anything else and understands Me to be present in all living entities, will obtain unflinching devotional service unto Me.

verses.sanskrit: भक्त्योद्धवानपायिन्या सर्व-लोक-महेश्वरम् । सर्वोत्पत्त्य्-अप्ययं ब्रह्म कारणं मोपयाति सः
verses.transliteration: bhaktyoddhavānapāyinyā sarva-loka-maheśvaram / sarvotpatty-apyayaṃ brahma kāraṇaṃ mopayāti saḥ
verses.translation: Oh Uddhava, he will come to Me by bhakti, by loving service unto Me, the Supreme Lord of All Worlds, the Absolute Truth and cause, the origin and dissolution of everything.

verses.sanskrit: इति स्व-धर्म-निर्णिक्त- सत्त्वो निर्ज्ञात-मद्-गतिः । ज्ञान-विज्ञान-सम्पन्नो न चिरात् समुपैति माम्
verses.transliteration: iti sva-dharma-nirṇikta- sattvo nirjñāta-mad-gatiḥ / jñāna-vijñāna-sampanno na cirāt samupaiti mām
verses.translation: When one thus by one's dharma has purified one's existence, fully understands My supreme position and is endowed with spiritual knowledge and wisdom, one will very soon reach Me.

verses.sanskrit: वर्णाश्रमवतां धर्म एष आचार-लक्षणः । स एव मद्-भक्ति-युतो निःश्रेयस-करः परः
verses.transliteration: varṇāśramavatāṃ dharma eṣa ācāra-lakṣaṇaḥ / sa eva mad-bhakti-yuto niḥśreyasa-karaḥ paraḥ
verses.translation: The followers of the varns'rama dharma are characterized by this [traditional] code of conduct. This sense of duty combined with my bhakti awards the highest perfection of life.

verses.sanskrit: एतत् ते ऽभिहितं साधो भवान् पृच्छति यच् च माम् । यथा स्व-धर्म-संयुक्तो भक्तो मां समियात् परम्
verses.transliteration: etat te 'bhihitaṃ sādho bhavān pṛcchati yac ca mām / yathā sva-dharma-saṃyukto bhakto māṃ samiyāt param
verses.translation: Oh saintly soul, this what I described to you on your request, constitutes the means by which one as a devotee can be perfectly engaged in one's duty, and thus come to Me, the One Supreme.'
